hero.headline: MUJ Online MBA in International Business: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved Super/Dual specialization. 12 spec subjects in Sem 3-4. NAAC A+, NIRF #58.

sections.tldr: MUJ Online MBA in International Business covers international financial management, global logistics, multinational corporation management, export-import finance, and foreign trade policy for professionals targeting global trade or international operations roles. NAAC A+, NIRF #58 at Rs 1,53,000 upfront (15% off) or Rs 1,80,000. Super/Dual spec — pair with Finance or Supply Chain. Coursera Plus at Rs 3,999 (70% off).

about.body:
International Business is the MBA specialization for professionals who manage global trade, export-import operations, multinational business strategy, and cross-border supply chains. The MUJ Online MBA in International Business covers the full scope of international commerce: international financial management, global logistics and distribution, MNC management, export-import management, foreign trade policy, and international business law.

MUJ's Super/Dual specialization gives International Business students flexible credential combinations. Pairing with Finance creates a strong international treasury and trade finance profile. Pairing with Supply Chain Management provides an end-to-end global logistics and customs operations credential. Both combinations are relevant for companies with significant export-import activity, MNC India operations, and logistics firms managing international freight.

The Coursera Plus bundle at Rs 3,999 gives International Business students access to global business strategy, trade finance, and cross-cultural management courses from Duke University and University of Virginia.

Manipal University Jaipur Online is NAAC A+ accredited with NIRF #58 University ranking for 2025. EdifyEdu verifies accreditation data independently and does not receive commission from MUJ. Full programme overview at /universities/manipal-university-jaipur-online/mba.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Global strategy frameworks, international competitive analysis, and strategy for multinational corporations.

sem3Subjects.name: Term Paper
sem3Subjects.description: Independent research paper applying international business theory to a real-world cross-border trade challenge.

sem3Subjects.name: International Financial Management
sem3Subjects.description: Currency risk management, cross-border capital structure, ADR/GDR structures, and international investment analysis.

sem3Subjects.name: International Marketing
sem3Subjects.description: Global market entry strategies, international product positioning, export pricing, and cross-cultural marketing.

sem3Subjects.name: Management of Multinational Corporations
sem3Subjects.description: MNC strategy, foreign direct investment decisions, global value chain management, and cross-border organisational design.

sem3Subjects.name: Export-Import Management
sem3Subjects.description: Export documentation, customs clearance, incoterms, trade compliance, and logistics for cross-border commerce.

sem4Subjects.name: International Business Management
sem4Subjects.description: Advanced international business strategy, regional trade blocs, and managing global operations at scale.

sem4Subjects.name: Project
sem4Subjects.description: Major dissertation applying international business learning to an original applied cross-border business study.

sem4Subjects.name: Foreign Trade of India
sem4Subjects.description: India's trade policy framework, EXIM policy, trade agreements (FTA/RCEP), and export promotion schemes.

sem4Subjects.name: Global Logistics and Distribution Management
sem4Subjects.description: International freight management, 3PL/4PL models, customs brokerage, and global distribution network design.

sem4Subjects.name: International Business Environment and International Law
sem4Subjects.description: WTO framework, bilateral trade agreements, international contract law, and dispute resolution in cross-border commerce.

sem4Subjects.name: Export-Import Finance
sem4Subjects.description: Letter of credit, bank guarantee, pre-shipment and post-shipment finance, and export credit insurance.

syllabus.note: Semester 1 and 2 subjects are shared across all MUJ Online MBA specializations. Full programme structure at /universities/manipal-university-jaipur-online/mba.

whoHires.body:
International Business MBA graduates from Manipal University Jaipur Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Export-import companies, MNC India operations, Customs and freight forwarding, EXIM banks and trade finance, Government trade bodies

**Entry-level roles:** Export Documentation Executive, International Trade Analyst, Global Supply Chain Coordinator, Trade Finance Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** International Business Manager, Head of Exports, Global Logistics Manager, Trade Finance Manager, Country Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Director of International Business, VP Global Operations, Head of Export Strategy, Chief Commercial Officer

**Top hiring organisations:** Tata International, Mahindra Logistics, EXIM Bank of India, DHL India, Maersk India, CII-EXIM, Reliance Industries exports division

skills.body:
The International Business specialization builds skills that export companies, MNCs, and trade finance institutions test at international business manager and director levels:

**Technical skills:**
- International Trade Finance: letter of credit management, SBLC, bank guarantees, export credit, FEMA compliance, and RBI trade finance regulations
- Export-Import Operations: customs documentation, HS code classification, incoterms application, and trade compliance management
- International Financial Management: FX hedging, cross-border treasury, transfer pricing, and repatriation of funds management
- MNC Strategy: global value chain analysis, FDI entry mode decision-making, and international joint venture management

**Applied skills:**
- Foreign Trade Policy: DGFT registration, export promotion council interactions, and leveraging India FTA benefits for import duty optimization
- Global Logistics: international freight mode selection, 3PL contract management, and port-of-entry risk management

**Soft skills:** Negotiating cross-border contracts with foreign counterparts, presenting export strategy to boards, managing multi-geography teams across different regulatory environments.

comparisons.body:
Comparing MUJ Online MBA International Business against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA International Business Management:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity IB covers international marketing and global strategy in depth. MUJ IB has stronger coverage of export-import operations, foreign trade policy, and trade finance instruments — practical differentiators for export-sector careers. MUJ's Rs 1,53,000 upfront fee is significantly lower. Amity NIRF #22 carries more weight in consulting and MNC management hiring.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA:** NMIMS does not offer a dedicated International Business specialization in its online MBA. NMIMS Finance covers international financial management as a subject. For professionals specifically targeting export-import or global trade careers, MUJ's dedicated IB spec is more relevant than NMIMS's general finance coverage.

**vs LPU Online MBA:** LPU is NAAC A++ with strong placement in north India. LPU does not offer a dedicated International Business spec in its online MBA. For international trade and export careers, MUJ's dedicated IB spec with export-import finance and foreign trade policy subjects is a clear differentiator.

fees.body:
The total program fee for the Manipal University Jaipur Online MBA in International Business can be paid through 2 options:

**Option 1 — Semester-wise payment**: Rs 45,000 per semester, Rs 1,80,000 total across 4 semesters
**Option 2 — Full upfront payment (discounted)**: Rs 1,53,000 one-time (Rs 1,80,000 standard less 15 percent fee concession)

A one-time registration fee of Rs 500 applies at enrolment. MUJ does not charge separate exam fees per attempt, project evaluation fees, or admission processing fees beyond this.

For working professionals, MUJ offers a 24-month zero-cost EMI plan at approximately Rs 7,500 per month on the standard fee (Rs 6,375 per month on the discounted fee) through its NBFC lending partners.

**Scholarships available** (non-stackable — pick one based on eligibility):
- **Divyaang** (persons with disabilities): 20% discount with Divyaang certificate
- **Defence personnel**: 20% discount with Service/IC number
- **Manipal University Jaipur alumni** (UG or PG): 20% discount
- **Government employees**: 10% discount with Govt ID
- **Merit scholarship**: 10% discount for 80%+ marks in 10+2 or bachelor's

**Coursera Plus bundled access** (MUJ differentiator): Enrolled MUJ students get Coursera Plus at Rs 3,999 (standard Rs 13,999 — a 70 percent discount). This includes 10,000+ courses from University of Virginia, University of Michigan, Johns Hopkins, University of California Irvine, and other top global universities.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees and scholarships with Manipal University Jaipur each quarter. Please reconfirm current offers and eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The Manipal University Jaipur degree certificate carries the Manipal University Jaipur name, the MBA designation, and identifies the online learning mode per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The International Business specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode identification on the certificate is standard for UGC-DEB approved programmes and equivalent in recognition to regular MBA degrees for employment, promotions, and further studies.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from MUJ Online MBA International Business alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Arjun Mehta
items.liked: Export-import finance and foreign trade policy subjects were directly applicable to my textile export company role
items.disliked: More coverage of India-specific FTA benefits (CEPA, SAFTA provisions) would have been useful for our export operations
items.body: I manage exports for a textile manufacturer. The export documentation, trade finance instruments, and DGFT provisions were covered at a practical level I had not encountered in other programmes. The foreign trade policy module gave me frameworks to optimize our export promotion scheme benefits. Paired International Business with Finance for a comprehensive export finance profile. The Manipal brand was recognised by our bank relationship manager.

items.name: Nisha Rao
items.liked: MNC management subject gave me frameworks for understanding how our company's global headquarters makes India investment decisions
items.disliked: Live sessions had timezone issues for international content — some professors teaching from different locations had audio problems
items.body: I work at an MNC India subsidiary managing business development. The MNC strategy and international financial management subjects were the most relevant. Understanding FDI decision frameworks and global value chains improved how I present business cases to our global headquarters. The dual specialization with Finance strengthened my treasury and international finance credentials. Manipal University Jaipur was recognised by my employer's global HR team.

items.name: Ravi Krishnamurthy
items.liked: Global logistics and distribution management content was practical for my freight forwarding company role
items.disliked: Placement support was weak for international trade roles specifically. Career services team had no contacts in customs, freight, or EXIM banking sectors
items.body: The content quality was adequate and the international trade focus was genuine. My frustration was placement support — the career portal had zero postings relevant to international trade or logistics. If you need placement assistance for export-import or freight forwarding roles, you will not get it from MUJ online career services. The Manipal brand helped with initial shortlisting at two logistics companies but the placement process was entirely self-directed.

faqs.question: Can I choose dual specialization with the MUJ Online MBA in International Business?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Super/Dual model allows you to pair International Business with a second specialization. Strong pairings include: IB plus Finance (for international treasury and trade finance roles at banks and MNCs), IB plus Supply Chain Management (for global logistics and import sourcing careers), or IB plus Marketing (for international brand management and export marketing). All combinations are included in the standard fee.

faqs.question: Is Coursera Plus access included or charged separately?
faqs.answer: Coursera Plus is available to enrolled MUJ students at Rs 3,999 per year (standard Rs 13,999 — 70% off). For International Business students, relevant Coursera offerings include global business strategy from Duke University, international trade law courses, and cross-cultural management programmes from University of Virginia.

faqs.question: What scholarships are available for the MUJ Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Five scholarship categories: 20% for Divyaang individuals, 20% for Defence personnel, 20% for MUJ alumni, 10% for Government employees, and 10% merit scholarship for 80%+ marks in 10+2 or bachelor's. These are non-stackable. Separately, upfront payment of the full fee gets a 15% fee concession, reducing the total from Rs 1,80,000 to Rs 1,53,000.

faqs.question: Is this MBA useful for professionals in the export-import and customs sector?
faqs.answer: Yes — export-import operations is one of the primary target profiles for this specialization. The export documentation, incoterms, trade finance, and DGFT subjects are directly applicable to professionals in export companies, customs brokerages, freight forwarding, and EXIM banking. Pairing with Finance adds trade finance depth; pairing with Supply Chain adds logistics operations coverage.

faqs.question: What salary can International Business MBA graduates from MUJ expect?
faqs.answer: International business managers and heads of exports with 3-6 years of experience and this MBA earn Rs 9-18 lakh at export companies and MNC India operations. Global logistics managers and country managers earn Rs 12-22 lakh. Director of International Business roles at large export-oriented companies pay Rs 20-40 lakh. Outcomes depend on industry (textiles vs chemicals vs engineering), company scale, and geography of trade focus.

chooseIf: You work in export-import, global trade, MNC India operations, or international freight and want a NAAC A+ MBA with dedicated coverage of export documentation, trade finance, and foreign trade policy under Rs 1.8 lakh.
You want to pair International Business with Finance or Supply Chain for a combined international trade and finance or logistics credential.
You can use Coursera Plus at Rs 3,999 to supplement with global business strategy and trade certifications from top international universities.
consider: You are targeting international consulting or global strategy roles at large MNCs where Amity NIRF #22 or NMIMS brand carries more weight.
You need the full-spectrum international business MBA for global management consulting — IMT Ghaziabad or IIFT's PGDIB has stronger employer recognition for international trade consulting roles.
You need placement support for export-import sector roles — MUJ online career services do not have sector-specific connections in customs, freight forwarding, or EXIM banking.
